dsfaModal.title: DPIA – {{name}}
dsfaModal.intro: Data Protection Impact Assessment acc. to Art. 35 GDPR. Required for high risks to rights and freedoms of natural persons.
dsfaModal.processingDescriptionPlaceholder: Type, scope, context and purpose of processing...
dsfaModal.necessityAssessmentPlaceholder: Why is processing necessary and proportionate?
dsfaModal.risksIdentifiedPlaceholder: What risks to the rights and freedoms of data subjects were identified?
dsfaModal.measuresTakenPlaceholder: What technical and organizational measures were taken?

hr.name: Personnel Administration (HR)
hr.purpose: Processing of employment relationships, payroll accounting, time tracking and personnel development.
hr.legal_basis: Art. 88 GDPR in conjunction with § 26 BDSG (Employee Data)
hr.data_categories: Master data (name, address), contact data, bank details, salary data, time tracking data, social security numbers.
hr.recipients: Social security agencies, tax office, banks (salary payment), payroll accounting.
hr.retention_period: 6-10 years according to statutory retention periods (HGB/AO).
hr.security_measures: Access controls, authorization concept (HR department only), encryption of transmissions.
hr.notes: Standard HR activity. Contains sensitive employee data.

crm.name: Customer Management & CRM
crm.purpose: Contract initiation, customer support, processing of services and billing.
crm.data_categories: Master data, contact details, contract data, payment details, correspondence history.
crm.data_subjects: Customers, prospects, contact persons for business customers.
crm.recipients: Banks (payment processing), CRM system host, internal accounting.
crm.retention_period: 10 years according to § 257 HGB and § 147 AO for invoices and accounting records.
crm.security_measures: Role-based permissions in the CRM, SSL/TLS encryption, password policy.

newsletter.name: Newsletter Marketing
newsletter.purpose: Sending newsletters, marketing emails, and product information.
newsletter.data_categories: Email address, name (optional), IP address (proof of opt-in), click and open behavior (anonymized).
newsletter.retention_period: Until revocation of consent (unsubscription), proof data for 3 years after unsubscription.
newsletter.security_measures: Double-Opt-In procedure, TLS encryption of forms, DPA with shipping provider.

hosting.name: Website Hosting & Logging
hosting.purpose: Provision of the website, error analysis, attack prevention, and system security.
hosting.legal_basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (Legitimate interest in operation and security)
hosting.data_categories: IP address, browser type, referrer URL, date/time of access, transferred data volume.
hosting.recipients: Web hosting provider, Content Delivery Network (CDN) if used.
hosting.retention_period: Server log files are deleted or anonymized after 7 days.
hosting.security_measures: HTTPS encryption (SSL/TLS), server hardening, DDoS protection, DPA with web host.

support.name: IT Support & Ticket System
support.purpose: Processing of support requests, elimination of technical malfunctions and user administration.
support.data_categories: Master data, email address, support content/tickets, system and diagnostic data, IP address.
support.recipients: Helpdesk software provider, IT support service provider.
support.retention_period: 3 years after closure of the ticket / resolution of the support request.
support.security_measures: Access restrictions on tickets, HTTPS encryption, audit trail for ticket changes.
support.notes: Operation of internal and external IT support.

cctv.name: Video Surveillance (CCTV)
cctv.purpose: Protection of buildings and persons, prevention and investigation of criminal offenses, house rules.
cctv.legal_basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (Legitimate interest in security and property protection)
cctv.data_categories: Image and video data of persons in the detection area (face, clothing, behavior).
cctv.data_subjects: Employees, visitors, customers, pedestrians in the monitored area.
cctv.recipients: Security service, law enforcement agencies (in the event of incidents), internal facility management.
cctv.retention_period: Automatic overwriting after 72 hours, in the event of incidents until the criminal complaint/clarification.
cctv.security_measures: Password-protected access to video system, encrypted storage, signs according to § 4 BDSG, access logging.
cctv.notes: Video surveillance is usually subject to DPIA according to Art. 35 GDPR. Involve the works council.

access_control.name: Access Control & ID Card System
access_control.purpose: Control and logging of physical access to buildings and security areas.
access_control.legal_basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (Legitimate interest in building security) in conjunction with § 26 BDSG (Employees)
access_control.data_categories: Personnel number, name, chip/card identifier, access times, access locations, biometric data (if used).
access_control.data_subjects: Employees, contractors, visitors with permanent ID.
access_control.recipients: Security officer, HR department, law enforcement agencies (in the event of incidents).
access_control.retention_period: Access logs: 6 months; in case of incidents until clarification; ID data: until termination of employment + 6 months.
access_control.security_measures: Encrypted database storage, role-based access to access logs, deletion concept, physical security of servers.
access_control.notes: When biometric data (fingerprint) is used, the legal basis changes to Art. 9(2)(b) GDPR — DPIA and works agreement required.

employee_monitoring.name: IT Usage Logging (Employee Monitoring)
employee_monitoring.purpose: Ensuring the proper use of operational IT resources, defense against cyber attacks, investigation of compliance violations.
employee_monitoring.legal_basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR in conjunction with § 26 BDSG (Employees) — only permissible in the event of concrete suspicion or random logging within the permitted framework
employee_monitoring.data_categories: Connection data (URLs, IP addresses), login times, email headers (no content), printer protocols, USB usage logs.
employee_monitoring.data_subjects: Employees, if applicable freelancers and contractors with system access.
employee_monitoring.recipients: IT security, CISO, HR department (in case of suspicion), works council.
employee_monitoring.retention_period: Standard protocol: 30 days; event-related evaluation: until conclusion of the procedure.
employee_monitoring.security_measures: Strictly regulated access (CISO + works council only), 4-eyes principle for evaluation, pseudonymized storage, audit-proof logging.
employee_monitoring.notes: DPIA and works agreement absolutely necessary. Involve the works council in advance (§ 87 Paragraph 1 No. 6 BetrVG).
